domain: Pistol shrimp cavitation latch-and-snap — claw stores elastic energy over msec, then snaps shut at 25 m/s producing cavitation bubble with plasma flash and 218 dB impulsive pressure pulse.
specific_mechanism: Slow energy accumulation (latch) + impulsive instantaneous release at threshold; brief high-amplitude pulse vs. continuous emission.
llm_application: SNAP-ATTN: phase-coherent attention burst via latch-accumulator. A coarse inter-layer accumulator sums normalized attention-score deltas across N layers. At threshold the next layer fires a single sharp-temperature (very low τ) burst attention on top-K tokens; subsequent layers reset accumulator and resume normal τ. Mechanism: slow store-up + impulsive sharp release of consolidated attention.
novelty_claim: SpikeLLM spikes individual neurons but evenly across time. Sharp-attention literature (e.g. anti-attention-sink) modulates per-layer τ globally. Adaptive τ schedulers tune τ along training. SNAP-ATTN's distinctive contribution is LATCH-ACCUMULATED across multiple layers + impulsive sharp-τ release at threshold-crossing layer — a multi-layer accumulator with discrete burst.
